scenario.title: Your European Tour
scenario.description: You're planning a laser show tour across 5 EU countries. Each country has different requirements for audience scanning.

interaction.instruction: Drag each country to its likely permit status:

feedback.correct: Excellent! You correctly identified that Germany and Austria require permits (Status A), Netherlands and Belgium don't require permits (Status D), and France indoors prohibits audience scanning (Status E).
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. Remember: Germany and Austria have strict permit requirements, while France indoors has a near-total ban on audience scanning.

phases.phase_name: Core Concept: The EU Regulatory Hierarchy

content.introduction: EU laser safety follows a three-tier hierarchy. Click each tier to explore:

content.description: Minimum health and safety requirements regarding worker exposure to artificial optical radiation
key_points: Applies to all EU member states
Employers must assess and manage optical radiation risks
Exposure limit values (ELVs) must not be exceeded
content.legal_basis: Article 153 of the Treaty on the Functioning of the European Union

content.description: European adoption of IEC 60825-1 for laser product safety
content.relationship: Links Directive requirements to technical specifications

content.description: Each EU member state implements through national laws and may add specific requirements
content.variation_note: Status classifications vary by country based on national enforcement practices

ce_marking_section.title: CE Marking Requirements
content: CE marking required for laser products sold in EU/EEA
Manufacturer issues EU Declaration of Conformity
Not a safety certification - it's a conformity mark

content.introduction: Explore the interactive map to understand specific country requirements. Select a country to see its Status classification and requirements.

countries.name: Finland
requirements: All lasers and techs must be listed in permit
Grating effects only (no scanned effects without special permit)

countries.name: Sweden
requirements: Permit required for all laser displays
Risk Assessment and Method Statement required
PASS or equivalent scan-fail device required

countries.name: Switzerland
requirements: 14 days advance notification via online portal
Level 2 required for audience exposure (includes MPE calculation knowledge)

certification.level_1: No laser in audience area (3m vertical or 2.5m lateral separation)
certification.level_2: Laser in audience area - requires MPE calculation and programming knowledge

countries.name: Netherlands
requirements: No specific laser display permit required
countries.notes: Professional practice: maintain 3m minimum beam height

countries.name: Belgium
requirements: No permit required for audience scanning

countries.name: France
requirements: Indoor: Audience scanning not allowed under almost all circumstances
Some regions prohibit all Class 4 laser devices
countries.notes: Suppliers may reclassify projectors to Class 2 to circumvent

countries.name: Poland
countries.notes: Situation unclear - permits rarely encountered in practice

countries.name: Luxembourg
countries.mpe_requirement: No radiation above MPE may touch living things

scenarios.title: Tour Planning
scenarios.description: You're planning a 3-city tour: Amsterdam, Stockholm, and Vienna. Which cities require advance permits?
scenarios.explanation: Stockholm (Sweden) and Vienna (Austria) are Status A - permits required. Amsterdam (Netherlands) is Status D - no permit required.

content.introduction: Test your mastery with these cross-border compliance challenges.

challenges.title: The Multi-Country Festival
challenges.description: You're providing lasers for a festival that tours Finland, Sweden, and Germany. Put the compliance steps in the correct order:

steps.text: Apply for permits from each country's radiation authority
Prepare Risk Assessments and Method Statements
Ensure all equipment has CE marking
steps.note: Prerequisite - should be done before permits

steps.text: Submit detailed equipment lists and tech credentials
Conduct on-site MPE measurements before each show

challenges.hint: Equipment compliance comes first, then permits, then documentation, then on-site preparation.

challenges.title: MPE Limit Challenge
challenges.description: Which country has the strictest MPE requirement for audience scanning?
challenges.explanation: Finland's STUK may grant special permits with MPE as low as 1 mW/cm², which is 10 times stricter than the standard 10 W/m².

achievement.message: Congratulations! You've mastered the EU regulatory framework.

interactive_elements.description: Clickable EU map showing Status A-F classifications
Collapsible pyramid showing EU directive hierarchy
